hero.title: Subscription Manager
hero.description: Track recurring services, renewal dates, categories, and subscription spend in one private manual workspace.

flow.title: Subscriptions → Categories → Renewals → Insights
steps: Create subscription records with provider, amount, frequency, and next billing date
Group services by category for clearer review
Record renewals manually and review spending summaries

differences.title: A private record system for recurring services
differences.description: Subscription Manager keeps recurring costs visible without connecting bank accounts, cards, inboxes, or provider APIs.

items.title: Track
items.description: Store each subscription with billing amount, frequency, renewal date, payment-method notes, and status.

items.title: Organize
items.description: Use categories and filters to separate personal, household, software, entertainment, and business subscriptions.

items.title: Review
items.description: Record renewals manually and review upcoming, overdue, trial, active, paused, and cancelled services.

feel.title: Clear renewal visibility without financial integrations
feel.description: The workspace is built for deliberate review: what renews, when it renews, and what it costs in each currency.

items.title: Private by default
items.description: All records are user-entered and owner-scoped.

items.title: Actionable
items.description: Statuses, dates, and cancellation notes make renewal decisions easier to revisit.

items.title: Transparent
items.description: Currency totals stay separated instead of pretending to convert values.

audiences.title: Who uses Subscription Manager
audiences.description: Subscription Manager is useful for people who need recurring-service visibility without account connections.

items.title: Individuals
items.description: Track personal services, trials, and renewal dates.

items.title: Families
items.description: Keep household subscriptions visible before shared costs surprise anyone.

items.title: Freelancers and small businesses
items.description: Review recurring software and service costs as operating commitments.

resourceSection.title: Subscription Manager essentials
resourceSection.description: V1 focuses on manual control and avoids risky automation scope.

items.title: Subscriptions
items.description: Create, edit, duplicate, pause, cancel, and delete subscription records.

items.title: Renewals
items.description: Record renewal events manually with amount, date, status, and next billing context.

items.title: Insights
items.description: Review active counts, trial counts, upcoming renewals, overdue renewals, and spending summaries.

groupedSection.title: A simple route from record keeping to review
groupedSection.description: The workflow starts with subscriptions and builds toward renewal visibility.

items.name: Subscriptions
items.description: Store provider, amount, frequency, status, next billing date, and reference details.

items.name: Renewals
items.description: Log each renewal decision or payment manually.

items.name: Insights
items.description: Summarize stored records by status, category, frequency, month, and currency.

technicalSection.title: Built within the Ansiversa platform model
technicalSection.description: Subscription Manager follows the shared mini-app architecture while keeping all domain records owner-scoped.
items: Protected workflow routes for Subscriptions, Categories, Renewals, and Insights.
Isolated backend database and Alembic migration.
Overview Explore CTAs route directly into the first real workflow route.

finalCta.title: Open Subscription Manager
finalCta.description: Start with the subscription list and review upcoming renewals from there.
